what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG handshake icon using currentColor strokes, no fill, rounded line caps, and rounded joins.
observations: Root svg sets viewBox, width, height, fill, stroke, stroke width, line cap, and line join attributes.
The icon is drawn with two path elements.
The main path forms an angular closed outline from left to right and back.
The second path adds a small central interlocking line detail.
No title, description, role, or aria attributes are present.
standouts: Uses currentColor so the icon inherits text or component color.
Uses a compact two-path construction for the handshake form.
conventions: Follows a line-icon convention with 24x24 sizing, no fill, 1.5 stroke width, and rounded stroke endings.
Keeps styling on the root svg rather than per-path except for fill on the first path.
Uses path geometry only, with no groups, symbols, masks, or embedded metadata.
concerns: No standalone accessibility label is included.
The first path explicitly repeats fill="none" even though the root svg already sets fill="none".
notes: A small stroke-based handshake asset intended for use as an inheritable-color interface icon.
questions: Should this icon be treated as decorative or given an accessible label by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: Filename suggests a handshake icon; the SVG implements a stylized handshake-like line icon.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a simple reusable handshake symbol that can adapt to surrounding UI color through currentColor.
novelty: The handshake is represented as angular connected segments rather than detailed hand shapes.
contribution: Provides a compact, themeable handshake icon asset that a fused system would keep as a reusable visual marker for agreement, partnership, or connection.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 currentColor SVG line icon named handshake.svg, drawn with two path elements and rounded stroke styling.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for supplying a reusable handshake interface icon in a design system asset set.
